Notice O Fmoiton And Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Requests For Admissions, Requests For Inspection And Production Of Documents, And Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-15-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
Alex P. Moskovitz, Esq., for Plaintiff and moving party: STEPHEN PORTARO
Carol C. Copsey, Esq., for Defendant and opposing party: Junior League of San Francisco, Inc. and Jackie Smith

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted. PLAINTIFF STEPHEN PORTARO's Motion To Compel Responses And Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Requests For Admissions, and Requests For Inspection And Production Of Documents is granted in part and denied in part.
The motion is granted as to Form Interrogatory 207.2 as Defendant agrees to supplement the response.
The motion is granted as to Request For Admission Nos. 1, 2, 16-20, 29, 30, 31, 32, 33, and 34.
The motion is denied as to Nos. 3, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15 as they are irrelevant and invade the right to privacy of a third party.
The motion is granted as to Nos. 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 21, 24, 25 as the objections are overruled and ordered stricken. The responses otherwise may be adequate.
The motion is denied as to Nos. 9, 22, 23 as they are irrelevant.
The motion is denied as to Nos. 26, 27, 28, and 35.
As to Request for Production Nos. 13-15, the motion is denied as they invade third parties' right to privacy without good cause, and they are overly broad.
The motion is granted as to Form Interrogatory 217.1. However, the responses are not required to identify other employees as their right to privacy is protected.
Further responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order.
All requests for sanctions are denied. Commissioner Everett A. Hewlett. Not reported.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-08-2010 Set For Trial On Aug-16-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Jul-28-10 At 03:30 P.M. In Dept 318
